# WikiKit â€” Full Documentation for LLMs

This single file contains the complete WikiKit documentation, inlined for LLM
consumption. A concise index is in `docs/llms.txt`.

WikiKit is a **headless, AI-native knowledge system for humans and agents**:
Markdown-first knowledge in, structured and agent-ready knowledge out. Sources
are archived verbatim, an LLM synthesizes maintained concept pages with
verifiable claims and citations, every change passes a human review gate as a
ChangeProposal, and the knowledge is reachable over HTTP/REST and MCP and
exportable as Markdown or OKF (Open Knowledge Format) bundles.

WikiKit's own operating and installation knowledge is an immutable built-in
system scope, shipped with the binary rather than seeded into a mutable user
space. Read it with `wikikit_guide`, `wikikit://system/agent-guide`, or
`GET /agent-guide.md`. User spaces remain reviewed, portable tenant data.

The guiding idea is curation over collection: WikiKit is not a dump of
everything you ever saw, it is a maintained set of concept pages an agent can
trust â€” every statement is a claim with a citation, a status and a lifecycle,
and nothing becomes visible without review. It is the knowledge foundation
that agents, dashboards and apps build on top of â€” WikiKit itself ships no CLI
and no web UI.

- Source: https://github.com/MikeBild/wikikit
- License: MIT

---

## 1. Core concept â€” the LLM-wiki loop

```
source (markdown | text | url)
  â”€â”€POST /v1/spaces/{space}/ingestâ”€â”€â–¶ archive verbatim (sha256 dedup)
  â”€â”€â–¶ classify (which concepts does this affect?)          [LLM, haiku]
  â”€â”€â–¶ synthesize new revision + claims + citations         [LLM, sonnet]
  â”€â”€â–¶ detect contradictions (declared-functional subject+predicate frames)
  â”€â”€â–¶ ONE pending ChangeProposal (staged rows, invisible to readers)
  â”€â”€â–¶ human reviews the structured diff and approves/rejects over REST
  â”€â”€â–¶ atomic apply: revision â†’ current, claims â†’ verified/disputed,
      space epoch bump, signed webhooks
```

Key invariants:

- **Sources are never mutated.** The archived `raw_content` is the provenance
  anchor; `sha256(content)` makes re-ingesting the same document idempotent
  (`409 already_ingested`).
- **Proposed content is invisible by construction.** Search, concept reads and
  exports join through `current_revision_id` and visible claim statuses â€”
  staged rows cannot leak.
- **Every LLM call is audited.** Model, prompt version, input hash and token
  usage land in an agent-run ledger and in the `agent_meta` of every generated
  row â€” revision history answers "which model wrote this, from which source?".
- **Classification is a revisable claim, not a schema.** Relations and
  categorizations are claims with a source, a status and a lifecycle â€” they
  can be disputed and deprecated like any other statement.
- **Approval remains a human decision, now available over MCP.** Agents stage
  with ingest/propose; a principal with `knowledge:review` reads the complete
  diff â€” including every staged decision with its context, rationale and
  alternatives â€” through `wikikit_proposals`. The agent passes only the
  proposal id to `wikikit_review_proposal`; WikiKit then asks the human for
  approve/reject and an optional note through native MCP form elicitation.

---

## 2. HTTP API

Base URL (self-host): `http://127.0.0.1:4060`. The live OpenAPI 3.1 document
is generated from the route registry and served at `/openapi.json` (committed
snapshot: `docs/openapi.json`). The `{space}` path segment is the space slug.

### Endpoints

| Method | Path                                          | Scope             | Purpose |
|--------|-----------------------------------------------|-------------------|---------|
| GET    | `/v1/spaces`                                  | knowledge:read    | List spaces visible to the current key |
| POST   | `/v1/spaces`                                  | admin             | Create a space (workspace scope for all knowledge) |
| GET    | `/v1/agent/briefing`                          | knowledge:read    | Compact, budgeted briefing for an ordered set of spaces |
| POST   | `/v1/agent/context`                           | knowledge:read    | Select task-relevant spaces from stable purpose metadata and return a compact briefing |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}`                          | knowledge:read    | Read a space (settings, epoch) |
| POST   | `/v1/spaces/{space}/settings`                 | admin             | Merge or replace stable purpose metadata used for task-context selection |
| POST   | `/v1/spaces/{space}/ingest`                   | knowledge:propose | Ingest a source (markdown\|text\|url) â€” async, `202` + `Location` to poll |
| POST   | `/v1/spaces/{space}/ingest/document`        | knowledge:propose | Upload a document (pdf\|docx\|xlsx\|md\|txt\|csv, raw body) â€” extracted to Markdown then ingested |
| POST   | `/v1/spaces/{space}/agent/sessions`           | knowledge:propose | Capture a coding-agent session: distil the rules a human taught â€” usually `no_learnings`, else one proposal |
| GET    | `/v1/ingests/{id}`                            | knowledge:propose | Ingest job status; done carries source_id and an optional proposal_id (null = no review work) |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/sources`                  | knowledge:read    | List archived sources (keyset pagination via `?before=`) |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/sources/{id}`             | knowledge:read    | Read one source (raw + normalized markdown) |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/source-streams`           | knowledge:read    | List connector source streams (sync contract): head pointer, latest version, tombstone state |
| DELETE | `/v1/spaces/{space}/source-streams/{external_source_id}` | knowledge:propose | Tombstone a source stream (idempotent soft delete â€” the upstream document is gone) |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/decisions`                | knowledge:read    | List decisions (active/superseded), newest first â€” the decision log |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/decisions/{slug}`         | knowledge:read    | Read one decision: context, decision, rationale, rejected alternatives |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/concepts`                 | knowledge:read    | List concepts (keyset `?after=`; ETag over the space epoch, 304 on If-None-Match) |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/concepts/{slug}`          | knowledge:read    | Read a concept: markdown + claims + citations + relations |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/concepts/{slug}/history`  | knowledge:read    | Revision history incl. agent_meta (model, prompt version, sources) |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/search`                   | knowledge:read    | LLM-free full-text search; ranked hits with `<mark>` headlines |
| POST   | `/v1/spaces/{space}/query`                    | knowledge:read    | Grounded Q&A with inline citations (LLM; 503 without a key) |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/proposals`                | knowledge:read \| knowledge:review | List change proposals (`?status=pending`) |
| POST   | `/v1/spaces/{space}/proposals`                | knowledge:propose | Stage a manual change proposal, incl. `relations_removed` (same review gate) |
| GET    | `/v1/proposals/{id}`                          | knowledge:read \| knowledge:review | Structured proposal diff incl. relations added/removed; `text/markdown` via Accept header |
| POST   | `/v1/proposals/{id}/approve`                  | knowledge:approve | Approve a pending proposal (atomic apply) |
| POST   | `/v1/proposals/{id}/reject`                   | knowledge:approve | Reject a pending proposal (staged rows kept for audit) |
| POST   | `/v1/proposals/{id}/split`                    | knowledge:review  | Split a pending proposal: full per-concept split, or defer a subset into one child |
| POST   | `/v1/proposals/{id}/request-changes`          | knowledge:review  | Terminal reject with a mandatory revision note â€” agents re-propose against the feedback |
| GET    | `/v1/proposals/{id}/lint`                     | knowledge:read    | Lint the STAGED content of one proposal (uncited claims, collisions, stale base, dangling links) |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/lint`                     | knowledge:read    | Knowledge health findings â€” LLM-free, CI-friendly |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/export`                   | knowledge:read    | Export the space as a zip bundle (`?format=md\|okf`) |
| POST   | `/v1/spaces/{space}/import`                   | knowledge:propose | Import a bundle (zip): sources archived, knowledge staged as ONE proposal |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/webhooks`                 | admin             | List webhook endpoints |
| POST   | `/v1/spaces/{space}/webhooks`                 | admin             | Register a webhook endpoint (Standard Webhooks; secret shown once) |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/webhooks/{id}/deliveries` | admin             | Delivery attempts for one endpoint (status, attempts, backoff) |
| GET    | `/v1/api-keys`                                | admin             | List visible API keys without plaintext or hashes |
| POST   | `/v1/api-keys`                                | admin             | Mint a scoped API key (plaintext shown once) |
| DELETE | `/v1/api-keys/{id}`                           | admin             | Revoke a DB-backed API key idempotently |
| GET    | `/v1/stats/mcp`                               | admin             | Global privacy-safe MCP session, protocol and tool usage |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/stats/http`              | knowledge:read    | HTTP calls, outcomes, latency/size and exact-window adoption |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/stats/usage`             | knowledge:read    | Cross-transport search/read/query/ingest/proposal behavior |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/stats/reviews`           | knowledge:read    | Cross-transport proposal inspection and review behavior |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/stats/ingests`           | knowledge:read    | Time-bucketed ingest lifecycle and duration aggregates |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/stats/knowledge`         | knowledge:read    | Time-bucketed knowledge growth and review activity |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/stats/llm`               | knowledge:read    | Time-bucketed LLM calls, tokens and duration |
| GET    | `/v1/spaces/{space}/stats/webhooks`          | knowledge:read    | Time-bucketed webhook events and outcomes |
| GET    | `/health`                                     | public            | Liveness â€” `200 "ok"` while the process is up |
| GET    | `/ready`                                      | public            | Readiness â€” `{status, version}`; 503 while draining |
| GET    | `/metrics`                                    | public            | Prometheus metrics |
| GET    | `/openapi.json`                               | public            | Generated OpenAPI 3.1 spec |
| GET    | `/review/{id}`                                | public            | Human review page for one proposal (content-free shell; data loads with the reviewer's key) |
| GET    | `/agent-guide.md`                             | public            | Built-in agent operating and no-CLI client setup guide |
| GET    | `/llms.txt`                                   | public            | Documentation index (llmstxt.org format) |
| GET    | `/llms-full.txt`                              | public            | This file |
| GET    | `/.well-known/llms.txt`                       | public            | Well-known alias of `/llms.txt` |
| GET    | `/.well-known/llms-full.txt`                  | public            | Well-known alias of `/llms-full.txt` |
| GET    | `/install.sh`                                 | public            | Agent hooks installer (macOS/Linux) â€” `curl -fsSL <host>/install.sh \| sh` wires Claude Code, Codex and Cursor |
| GET    | `/install.ps1`                                | public            | Agent hooks installer (Windows, PowerShell 5.1) â€” `irm <host>/install.ps1 \| iex` |
| GET    | `/install/hooks/{script}`                     | public            | One lifecycle hook script (closed set of six: briefing/context/capture as `.sh` and `.ps1`) |

MCP (Streamable HTTP) is mounted at POST /mcp â€” intentionally outside the
REST/OpenAPI surface. Local clients use scoped API keys; remote MCP clients
use OAuth 2.1 discovery, PKCE and explicitly requested scopes (Â§7).

The stats resources are product-owned analytics, not a consumer-specific
reporting interface. The original ingests/knowledge/LLM/webhook readers derive
operational and growth metrics from durable product tables. The opt-in usage
ledger additionally measures actual HTTP and MCP connections/calls plus
search/read/query/ingest/proposal/review behavior. Space readers reuse normal
space-aware `knowledge:read`; global MCP aggregation requires `admin`.

All usage responses use schema `wikikit.usage-stats.v1`. Query parameters are
`bucket=hour|day|month|year`, RFC3339 `from`/`to`, `tz=UTC`,
`traffic_class=organic|synthetic|internal|all`, and at most two comma-separated
surface-specific `group_by` dimensions. Totals run a separate full-window SQL
query, so unique actors/sessions and percentiles are exact instead of sums of
buckets. Metric values declare `value_kind`, `value_state`, sample size and,
for ratios, numerator/denominator. `quality` declares `sampled:false`, exact
unique counting, retention/dropped events and `content_captured:false`.

Collection is off by default. Enable `WIKIKIT_USAGE_TELEMETRY_ENABLED` only
with an independent `WIKIKIT_USAGE_HMAC_SECRET`. Stored events contain
controlled operation/route/tool names, outcome/status, traffic/source,
duration/size/count/capacity and product-local HMAC actor/session ids. They
never contain content, prompts, search/question text, MCP arguments/results,
raw paths/query strings, IP/UA, credentials, e-mail, space slugs or dynamic
resource ids. Anonymous HTTP is never fingerprinted; health/docs/stats traffic
is internal; raw rows expire after `WIKIKIT_USAGE_RETENTION_DAYS`. Cross-product
actor joins are forbidden. Use `/v1/spaces/{space}/lint` for current data quality.

### Conventions

- **Request IDs:** every response carries a 12-hex `x-request-id` header; the
  same id appears in error envelopes and logs.
- **Trace context:** valid W3C `traceparent` trace ids are continued with a new
  server span; every response and structured request log carries `trace_id`,
  `span_id` and `parent_span_id`.
- **Pagination is keyset:** `?limit=&after=` (opaque cursor); responses carry
  `next_after: string | null` (sources paginate backwards with `?before=` and
  `next_before`).
- **Caching:** concept lists carry `ETag: "<space-epoch>"`; the epoch bumps on
  every approved proposal, so `If-None-Match` gives cheap 304s on any read of
  approved knowledge.
- **Async ingest:** `POST .../ingest` answers `202` with
  `{ingest_id, status:"queued"}` and `Location: /v1/ingests/{id}`; poll until
  `done` (carries `source_id`; `proposal_id` is nullable when no affected or new
  knowledge requires review) or `failed` (carries `error.code/message`).

### Examples

Ingest a note and poll:

```bash
curl -s -X POST "$WIKIKIT_URL/v1/spaces/default/ingest" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer wk_..." -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"markdown":"# OKF\nOKF is a draft spec for knowledge bundles.","title":"OKF note","source_kind":"note"}'
# â†’ 202 {"ingest_id":"...","status":"queued"}  + Location: /v1/ingests/{id}

curl -s "$WIKIKIT_URL/v1/ingests/<id>" -H "Authorization: Bearer wk_..."
# â†’ {"ingest_id":"...","status":"done","proposal_id":"...","source_id":"...","error":null}
```

Review the diff (human-readable) and approve:

```bash
curl -s "$WIKIKIT_URL/v1/proposals/<id>" -H "Accept: text/markdown" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer wk_..."          # chat-readable structured diff

curl -s -X POST "$WIKIKIT_URL/v1/proposals/<id>/approve" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer wk_..." -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"note":"source is current"}'
```

Search (LLM-free) vs. query (LLM synthesis with citations):

```bash
curl -s "$WIKIKIT_URL/v1/spaces/default/search?q=okf" -H "Authorization: Bearer wk_..."
curl -s -X POST "$WIKIKIT_URL/v1/spaces/default/query" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer wk_..." -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"question":"Is OKF production ready?"}'
```

Lint as a CI gate:

```bash
curl -s "$WIKIKIT_URL/v1/spaces/default/lint" -H "Authorization: Bearer wk_..." \
  | jq -e '[.findings[] | select(.severity=="error")] | length == 0'
```

---

## 3. Authentication & scopes

API keys look like `wk_<43 chars base64url>`; the plaintext is returned exactly
once by `POST /v1/api-keys` and stored only as an HMAC-SHA256 hash keyed with
`WIKIKIT_KEY_PEPPER`. Send a key as `Authorization: Bearer wk_...` or
`X-API-Key: wk_...`.

Scopes:

| Scope               | Grants |
|---------------------|--------|
| `knowledge:read`    | spaces, sources, concepts, history, search, query, proposals (read), lint, export |
| `knowledge:propose` | ingest + ingest status, manual proposals, import |
| `knowledge:review` | inspect proposals (REST GET proposals too) and start the MCP review where the human decides (implied by `knowledge:approve`) |
| `knowledge:approve` | approve/reject proposals over REST â€” the human-operator review credential; implies `knowledge:review` |
| `admin`             | spaces (create), webhooks, api-keys; implies all knowledge scopes |
| `*`                 | bootstrap key â€” everything |

Keys can be **space-scoped**: a key bound to one space gets
`403 insufficient_scope` on any other space. Two distinct failure modes:
`401 unauthorized` = the key was not recognized (missing/revoked/wrong pepper);
`403 insufficient_scope` = recognized key, missing scope or wrong space.

In dev, when no admin key exists at boot, a bootstrap key with scope `*` is
generated and printed **once** to stdout (pin it via
`WIKIKIT_BOOTSTRAP_API_KEY`). Recommended production key split: a read key for
consumers, a propose key for agents, an approve key for reviewers â€” never hand
the approve scope to an autonomous agent.

---

## 4. Domain model

| Entity | What it is |
|--------|------------|
| Space | Workspace scope; carries `settings` (incl. the controlled predicate vocabulary) and an `epoch` that bumps on every approved proposal |
| Source | An archived original document (`markdown\|text\|url\|import`), verbatim + normalized markdown, `sha256` content hash = idempotency anchor |
| Concept | A wiki page identity (slug) whose content lives in immutable revisions; `current_revision_id` points at the approved one |
| Revision | Immutable page version: `proposed â†’ current â†’ superseded` (or `rejected`); carries `agent_meta` `{model, prompt_version, input_hash, usage, source_ids}` |
| Claim | A verifiable statement `subject / predicate / object` with `confidence`, citations, and status `proposed â†’ verified â†’ disputed â†’ deprecated` |
| Citation | Claim provenance: source + verbatim quote + locator |
| Relation | Concept link: `related \| part_of \| depends_on \| contradicts \| supersedes`; added via `concepts[].relations`, removed via a proposal's top-level `relations_removed` (approval deactivates the edge, soft delete) |
| Decision | A first-class recorded decision: context, decision, rationale, alternatives (the decision-log pattern; meeting sources are mined for these) |
| ChangeProposal | The review gate: staged rows in the target tables (never a JSON diff blob) that flip atomically on approve |
| Ingest job | Async pipeline run: `queued â†’ running â†’ done\|failed\|quota_blocked` |
| Agent run | LLM audit ledger entry: kind, model, prompt_version, input_hash, usage, duration |

Contradiction handling is deterministic and cardinality-aware: only predicates
listed in a space's `settings.functional_predicates` treat different objects
for the same `(subject, predicate)` as a conflict. Undeclared predicates are
multi-valued. On a declared-functional collision both sides flip to `disputed`
and a `contradicts` relation is ensured.

Stale-base protection: every proposed revision remembers the revision it was
synthesized against; approving a proposal whose base is no longer current
fails with `409 stale_base` instead of clobbering newer knowledge.

---

## 5. Ingest pipeline & LLM usage

```
acquire â†’ archive â†’ dedup-gate â†’ classify â†’ synthesize (per concept)
        â†’ contradiction-detect â†’ propose (one transaction) â†’ webhook
```

- **Acquire:** markdown/text in the request body, or `{url}` (HTML is
  normalized to Markdown; raw archived verbatim). An optional `source_kind`
  (`meeting | article | note`) is recorded on the source and steers synthesis â€”
  `meeting` turns on decision mining.
- **Classify** (`WIKIKIT_MODEL_CLASSIFY`, default `claude-haiku-4-5`): one call
  with the source + a compact concept index â†’ which existing concepts are
  affected, which new concepts are warranted.
- **Synthesize** (`WIKIKIT_MODEL_SYNTHESIS`, default `claude-sonnet-5`): one
  call per affected concept â€” current revision + source â†’ new markdown,
  summary, claims with quotes and confidences, relations, and (for `meeting`
  sources) explicit decisions staged as proposed `wk_decisions`. Categorizations
  are emitted as claims, not fixed taxonomy, so a later source can dispute them.
  Prompts are versioned (`synthesize.v1`); a shipped prompt change adds a new
  version.
- **Answer** (`WIKIKIT_MODEL_ANSWER`, default `claude-sonnet-5`): grounded Q&A
  for `POST .../query` â€” answers only from retrieved evidence, cites inline,
  flags disputed claims, and says "not in the knowledge base" on empty
  retrieval instead of hallucinating.

The LLM runs through the Vercel AI SDK; the provider is config-selected
(`WIKIKIT_LLM_PROVIDER` = `anthropic` (default) / `openai` / `google`) and only
its API key has no default. Without a key every LLM-free feature (search, read,
history, lint, export, import, review) keeps working; ingest and query answer
`503 llm_not_configured` with actionable hints. Anthropic prompt caching is
wired (the static system prompt is a cache-controlled prefix), so repeated
synthesis pays for the cached prefix at ~0.1x. `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` is honored
for the anthropic provider (test stubs).

---

## 6. Error envelope

Every non-2xx JSON response (and every MCP tool error) is a terminal,
actionable envelope â€” never a bare string:

```json
{
  "error": "human-readable message",
  "code": "machine_code",
  "request_id": "a1b2c3d4e5f6",
  "next_best_actions": ["optional short imperative hints"]
}
```

| Code | HTTP | Meaning |
|------|------|---------|
| `bad_request` | 400 | request failed schema validation (zod details in `error`) |
| `unauthorized` | 401 | missing, unknown or revoked API key |
| `insufficient_scope` | 403 | known key, missing scope or wrong space |
| `not_found` | 404 | resource does not exist |
| `already_ingested` | 409 | content-hash hit; envelope carries `source_id` |
| `proposal_not_pending` | 409 | proposal already reviewed |
| `stale_base` | 409 | concept changed since the proposal was synthesized |
| `approval_requires_human` | 400 | `decision`/`note` passed as review tool input; the decision belongs to the human; proposal is unchanged |
| `elicitation_not_supported` | 409 | form capability vanished mid-review (backstop; the tool normally returns a `human_review_required` hand-off first); proposal is unchanged |
| `invalid_elicitation_response` | 400 | human form remained invalid after one bounded retry; proposal is unchanged |
| `elicitation_timeout` | 408 | human form timed out; proposal is unchanged |
| `elicitation_failed` | 502 | client or transport failed before a valid human decision; proposal is unchanged |
| `body_too_large` | 413 | body exceeds `WIKIKIT_MAX_BODY_BYTES` |
| `rate_limited` | 429 | slow down |
| `internal_error` | 500 | unexpected failure (message never leaks internals) |
| `llm_not_configured` | 503 | set the key for the configured `WIKIKIT_LLM_PROVIDER` to enable ingest/query (the error names it) |
| `draining` | 503 | graceful shutdown in progress |

---

## 7. MCP server

Streamable HTTP at `POST /mcp` (plus GET/DELETE for SSE/session close), built
on `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`. Local/operator clients use the same `wk_` key
auth as REST. Remote interactive clients use OAuth 2.1 discovery, DCR,
authorization code + PKCE, rotating refresh tokens and Bearer access tokens.
An unauthenticated `/mcp` response advertises protected-resource metadata via
`WWW-Authenticate`; OAuth tokens expose only the consented read/propose/approve
scopes, capped by the configured identity-provider policy.

Remote human login is configured only by the provider-neutral
`WIKIKIT_OAUTH_PROVIDERS` JSON list. It accepts one API-key plus multiple named
direct OIDC adapters concurrently; product names are configuration values.
WikiKit owns each OIDC client, callback, secret and policy; no cross-product
authentication runtime is involved. Each OIDC adapter requires the immutable
`sub` claim and applies an explicit exact-subject or provider-verified-email
allow-list. Email is optional and unverified email is ignored. The interactive permission ceiling
defaults to `knowledge:read,knowledge:propose`; add `knowledge:review` for
review-starting agents and `knowledge:approve` only
for trusted human reviewers, and the client must request it explicitly. OAuth
never issues `admin`. A connector stores the tools and scopes it scanned, so a
new tool or scope requires a rescan/reconnect rather than silently broadening
an old token.

The browser chooser implements `mcp-auth-v2` and always renders `Continue with
SSO` before `Continue with API key`; configured labels never change those
actions. Authorize redirects through the generic
`/v1/identity/login/start?login_state=<opaque>` funnel. Provider discovery is
`GET /v1/identity/providers`; assertion exchange is
`POST /v1/identity/sessions` with `{provider_id,identity_token}` and the exact
response `{api_key,principal_id,context_id,email}`; `email` is nullable and
contains only a provider-verified address. Only the generic
start/callback/logout routes existâ€”there are no provider-named or legacy
aliases. WikiKit returns `context_id:null` and keeps space authorization in the
issued key.

Sessions are leases: idle TTL sweep (`WIKIKIT_MCP_SESSION_TTL_MS`), hard cap
(`WIKIKIT_MCP_MAX_SESSIONS`) with oldest-idle eviction; a different key on a
known session id gets 404 (hijack guard); an unknown session id gets JSON-RPC
`-32001` so clients re-initialize cleanly. Origin headers are validated
(DNS-rebinding guard) and `mcp-protocol-version` is checked against the SDK's
supported versions.

**Scope-gating = tool visibility**: `tools/list` returns only tools whose
scope the key holds â€” a `knowledge:read` key sees only the read tools.
`wikikit_proposals` and `wikikit_review_proposal` require `knowledge:review`
(implied by `knowledge:approve`); the REST approve/reject endpoints still
demand `knowledge:approve`, so a review-scoped agent key can never approve
over HTTP. The review tool accepts only `{proposal_id}` â€” passing
`decision` or `note` as arguments is refused with `approval_requires_human`
before anything else happens. On a form-capable client it issues a native
`elicitation/create` form for the human-owned decision and optional note.
Accepted forms perform the atomic write and persist
`review_channel:"mcp_elicitation"`; decline, cancel, timeout, invalid data or
transport failure performs no mutation. The POST response stays on the
event-stream transport so the server can originate the form request while the
tool call is in flight.

On a client without `capabilities.elicitation.form` the tool performs no
mutation and returns a hand-off instead of an error:
`{proposal_id, status:"pending", outcome:"human_review_required",
review_url, mutation_applied:false, poll_with:"wikikit_proposals",
agent_instructions}`. The `agent_instructions` are scope-matched. A
review-scoped key hands the user the `review_url` â€” the embedded review page
at `GET /review/{id}`, where the human authenticates with their own reviewer
key and decides â€” and polls `wikikit_proposals`; it never collects
approve/reject in chat and never calls the REST review endpoints on the
human's behalf. A key the operator deliberately granted `knowledge:approve`
is additionally instructed that it may execute the user's clearly stated
approve/reject instruction over REST, quoting it in the audit note.

Elicitation is synchronous and bounded by
`WIKIKIT_MCP_ELICITATION_TIMEOUT_MS`. The pending proposal is already the
durable workflow object, so WikiKit deliberately adds no MCP task, external
elicitation URL or dialog lease. A reviewer can retry later.

**Coding-agent loop** (`docs/coding-agent-integration.md`): an optional
SessionStart hook injects only a compact briefing, and UserPromptSubmit selects
task-relevant additional spaces from stable space-purpose metadata. A
SessionEnd/Stop hook
posts the transcript to `POST /v1/spaces/{space}/agent/sessions`, which distils
ONLY durable rules a human explicitly taught and stages them as a proposal.
A routine session answers `no_learnings` and writes nothing; the transcript is
distilled and dropped, never archived. Claude Code, Codex and Cursor expose
equivalent lifecycle events; example hook scripts ship as `.sh`/`.ps1` pairs
(`examples/agent-hooks/`, scripts `wikikit-briefing`/`wikikit-context`/
`wikikit-capture`) and every server serves its own agent hooks installer at
`GET /install.sh` (macOS/Linux) and `GET /install.ps1` (Windows) â€” unrelated
to the repository's git pre-push `hooks:install`.

**Self-description**: `initialize` returns `instructions` (how the tools fit
together; the staging-not-publishing rule), and the server advertises a
`resources` capability. `wikikit_guide` exposes the compact system guide even
to tools-only clients. `resources/list` offers the same guide at
`wikikit://system/agent-guide` plus this documentation at
`wikikit://docs/llms.txt` and `wikikit://docs/llms-full.txt`, readable via
`resources/read`, so an MCP-only client never needs an HTTP GET to learn the
API. Resources are not scope-gated; the same files are public over HTTP.

### Tools

| Tool | Scope | Behavior | readOnly | destructive | idempotent | openWorld |
|------|-------|----------|----------|-------------|------------|-----------|
| `wikikit_guide` | knowledge:read | Read code-versioned system knowledge and no-CLI client setup; no database seed | true | false | true | false |
| `wikikit_spaces` | knowledge:read | Discover spaces visible to the current key | true | false | true | false |
| `wikikit_briefing` | knowledge:read | Compact budgeted orientation for an ordered set of spaces | true | false | true | false |
| `wikikit_context` | knowledge:read | Select relevant spaces from the current task, or honor an explicit manual space list | true | false | true | false |
| `wikikit_search` | knowledge:read | Ranked FTS hits (raw evidence, no synthesis) | true | false | true | false |
| `wikikit_read` | knowledge:read | Full concept: markdown + claims + citations + relations + rev | true | false | true | false |
| `wikikit_sources` | knowledge:read | Provenance chain down to original sources (by concept slug or source_id) | true | false | true | false |
| `wikikit_decisions` | knowledge:read | Decision log: one decision by slug, or list all active/superseded | true | false | true | false |
| `wikikit_history` | knowledge:read | Revisions incl. agent_meta (model, prompt version) | true | false | true | false |
| `wikikit_lint` | knowledge:read | Knowledge health findings | true | false | true | false |
| `wikikit_ingest` | knowledge:propose | Submit a source â†’ async ack `{status:"running", ingest_id, poll_with:"wikikit_ingest_status"}` | false | true | true | true |
| `wikikit_ingest_status` | knowledge:propose | Poll an ingest job; terminal: done (proposal_id) / failed (error) | true | false | true | false |
| `wikikit_propose` | knowledge:propose | Stage a structured ChangeProposal â†’ `{proposal_id, status:"pending"}` | false | true | true | false |
| `wikikit_proposals` | knowledge:review | List proposals or inspect one complete structured diff | true | false | true | false |
| `wikikit_review_proposal` | knowledge:review | Input `{proposal_id}` only; native human form owns the decision; accepted review writes, decline/cancel are no-ops; no form capability â†’ `human_review_required` hand-off with `review_url`; `decision`/`note` as input â†’ `approval_requires_human` | false | true | false | false |

Annotation rationale (fixed): writes are `destructiveHint: true` even though
they only stage content â€” an honest write is a write. `idempotentHint: true`
on the writes because content-hash dedup (ingest) and the pending input-hash
unique index (propose) make identical retries converge. `openWorldHint: true`
only on `wikikit_ingest` (a `url` input fetches an external host).

Connect any MCP host over Streamable HTTP at `https://<host>/mcp`, using OAuth
or a narrow Bearer `wk_` key with the required knowledge scopes.

For Codex, set
`approval_policy = { granular = { mcp_elicitations = true } }` and
`approvals_reviewer = "user"`. Claude Code requires 2.1.76 or newer. ChatGPT
review support is conditional on the active connector advertising native form
elicitation; reconnect and run a canary after deployment. Without that
capability the review hands off (`human_review_required` with a
`review_url`): the human opens the review page and decides there as
themselves, and the agent reports the outcome from `wikikit_proposals`.

---

## 8. Webhooks (Standard Webhooks)

State changes write a transactional outbox row; a delivery worker fans out to
matching endpoints with exponential backoff (up to
`WIKIKIT_WEBHOOK_MAX_ATTEMPTS`) and a circuit breaker
(`WIKIKIT_WEBHOOK_CIRCUIT_THRESHOLD` consecutive failures â†’ endpoint paused
15 min). Endpoint secrets are `whsec_`-style, shown once at registration.

Events:

```
wikikit.proposal.created    {proposal_id, space, title, source_ids, concepts, claims_count, contradictions_count, relations_removed_count}
wikikit.proposal.approved   {proposal_id, space, reviewer, note, review_channel, concepts}
wikikit.proposal.rejected   {proposal_id, space, reviewer, note, review_channel}
wikikit.concept.updated     {space, slug, rev, proposal_id}
wikikit.ingest.failed       {ingest_id, space, error: {code, message}}
```

Envelope: HTTP POST `application/json` with Standard Webhooks headers â€”
`webhook-id` (`evt_<n>`), `webhook-timestamp` (unix seconds),
`webhook-signature` (`v1,<base64(hmacSHA256(secret, "{id}.{timestamp}.{body}"))>`).
Body: `{"type": "...", "timestamp": "<ISO 8601>", "data": {...}}`. Always
verify the signature (constant-time; the header may carry several
space-separated signatures during secret rotation). This is the standard seam
any system (n8n, a governance layer, anything) reacts through â€” no code
dependency.

---

## 9. Export / import (Markdown & OKF)

`GET /v1/spaces/{space}/export?format=md|okf` streams a zip; identical
knowledge always zips to identical bytes (deterministic snapshot + fixed file
order).

**Markdown tree** (`format=md`, Obsidian-friendly):

```
index.md               TOC with [[slug]] wiki links (derived, regenerated)
log.md                 audit narrative: proposal, reviewer, review channel, model (derived)
concepts/<slug>.md     claims + relations in YAML frontmatter, body = markdown
decisions/<slug>.md    context/decision/rationale/alternatives in frontmatter
sources/<hash>.md      kind/url/title in frontmatter, body = raw verbatim
```

Claims round-trip losslessly through frontmatter (see
`examples/concept-frontmatter-reference.md`). Citations reference sources by
content hash â€” ids never travel across systems.

**OKF bundle** (`format=okf`): conforms to the vendored Open Knowledge Format
v0.1 spec (`docs/okf-v0.1.md`); every manifest carries `okf_version: "0.1"`
plus a generator stamp. WikiKit-specific structure (claims, relations) rides
an extension block, so foreign OKF consumers still read the documents.

**Import** (`POST /v1/spaces/{space}/import?format=md|okf`, body = zip):
sources are archived directly; concepts/claims/decisions are staged as **one
ChangeProposal** â€” foreign knowledge passes the same review gate as LLM
output. Claim/decision status is re-derived by the gate on approve; review
history does not travel.

---

## 10. Knowledge lint

`GET /v1/spaces/{space}/lint` â€” LLM-free SQL checks, CI-consumable
(`{findings: [{rule, severity, message, concept_slug?, claim_id?, details?}], counts}`):

| Rule | Severity |
|------|----------|
| `contradictions` | error |
| `missing-citations` | error |
| `broken-relations` | error |
| `stale-claims` | warn |
| `orphan-concepts` | warn |
| `empty-concepts` | info |
| `unreviewed-proposals` | info |
| `dangling-sources` | info |

---

## 11. Configuration (environment variables)

All config is via env vars (`WIKIKIT_*` prefix; optional `.env`; committed
`.env.defaults` supplies dev values and is **never** read when
`NODE_ENV=production`). Precedence: process env > `.env` > `.env.defaults`.
Invalid values fail the boot, not the request.

| Variable | Purpose | Default |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `HOST` | bind address | `127.0.0.1` |
| `PORT` | HTTP listen port | `4060` |
| `WIKIKIT_PUBLIC_URL` | canonical OAuth issuer/resource + MCP origin allowlist; HTTPS in production | `http://127.0.0.1:4060` |
| `DATABASE_URL` | Postgres connection string; **required in production** | dev: local Docker Postgres |
| `WIKIKIT_KEY_PEPPER` | HMAC pepper for API-key hashes; **required in production** | dev: committed dev pepper |
| `WIKIKIT_BOOTSTRAP_API_KEY` | pin the bootstrap admin key | (dev: generated + printed once) |
| `DEPLOYMENT_ENVIRONMENT` | deployment identity in structured logs | `development` outside production |
| `WIKIKIT_LLM_PROVIDER` | LLM provider the AI SDK routes to: `anthropic`/`openai`/`google` | `anthropic` |
| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | key for the `anthropic` provider; enables ingest/query | (unset â†’ 503 `llm_not_configured`) |
| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | key for the `openai` provider | (unset) |
| `GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY` | key for the `google` provider | (unset) |
| `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` | Anthropic API base (test stubs); honored when provider is `anthropic` | (empty) |
| `WIKIKIT_MODEL_SYNTHESIS` | synthesis model | `claude-sonnet-5` |
| `WIKIKIT_MODEL_CLASSIFY` | cheap/filter model: classification + session distillation | `claude-haiku-4-5` |
| `WIKIKIT_MODEL_ANSWER` | grounded-answer model | `claude-sonnet-5` |
| `WIKIKIT_EMBEDDING_PROVIDER` | optional hybrid-retrieval embedding provider: `none`/`openai`/`google` | `none` (lexical-only) |
| `WIKIKIT_MODEL_EMBEDDING` | embedding model (must produce 1536-dim vectors) | `text-embedding-3-small` |
| `WIKIKIT_MAX_BODY_BYTES` | max request body â†’ 413 | `10485760` (10 MiB) |
| `WIKIKIT_MAX_INGEST_TOKENS` | chunking threshold for large sources | `100000` |
| `WIKIKIT_INGEST_CONCURRENCY` | parallel ingest workers (1â€“16) | `2` |
| `WIKIKIT_INGEST_LEASE_MS` | worker lease before `worker_lost` reaping | `900000` |
| `WIKIKIT_INGEST_HEARTBEAT_MS` | lease renewal cadence (< half the lease) | `30000` |
| `WIKIKIT_WEBHOOK_POLL_MS` | outbox poll interval | `5000` (dev file: `1000`) |
| `WIKIKIT_WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_MS` | per-delivery HTTP timeout | `10000` |
| `WIKIKIT_WEBHOOK_MAX_ATTEMPTS` | retries before a delivery is dead | `10` |
| `WIKIKIT_WEBHOOK_CIRCUIT_THRESHOLD` | consecutive failures â†’ endpoint paused 15 min | `5` |
| `WIKIKIT_WEBHOOK_ALLOW_PRIVATE` | allow private/loopback webhook targets (SSRF guard) | `true` outside production |
| `WIKIKIT_TRUST_PROXY` | trust `X-Forwarded-*` (behind Caddy/nginx) | `false` |
| `WIKIKIT_MCP_SESSION_TTL_MS` | MCP idle session TTL | `1800000` (30 min) |
| `WIKIKIT_MCP_MAX_SESSIONS` | MCP session hard cap (oldest-idle eviction) | `200` |
| `WIKIKIT_MCP_ELICITATION_TIMEOUT_MS` | native human review-form timeout; fails closed (10 sâ€“30 min) | `300000` (5 min) |
| `WIKIKIT_USAGE_TELEMETRY_ENABLED` | enable privacy-bounded usage collection | `false` |
| `WIKIKIT_USAGE_HMAC_SECRET` | independent product-local HMAC secret; required when enabled | (unset) |
| `WIKIKIT_USAGE_RETENTION_DAYS` | raw usage retention (31â€“365 days) | `90` |
| `WIKIKIT_OAUTH_DCR_ENABLED` | RFC 7591 dynamic registration for remote MCP clients | `true` |
| `WIKIKIT_OAUTH_CODE_TTL_MS` | authorization-code TTL | `600000` (10 min) |
| `WIKIKIT_OAUTH_ACCESS_TOKEN_TTL_MS` | access-token TTL | `3600000` (1 h) |
| `WIKIKIT_OAUTH_REFRESH_TOKEN_TTL_MS` | rotating refresh-token TTL | `2592000000` (30 d) |
| `WIKIKIT_OAUTH_ALLOWED_SCOPES` | interactive identity permission ceiling | `knowledge:read,knowledge:propose` |
| `WIKIKIT_OAUTH_ENABLE_SIGNUP` | auto-admit unknown OIDC identities at the SSO callback with the minimal `knowledge:read` ceiling | `false` |
| `WIKIKIT_OAUTH_PROVIDERS` | WikiKit-local browser-provider configuration; JSON records use `protocol: api_key` or `oidc` | API-key record |
| `LOG_LEVEL` | `debug`/`info`/`warn`/`error` | `info` |
| `NODE_ENV` | `production` activates guards + disables `.env.defaults` | (unset) |

Zero-config principle: `./wikikit` with nothing configured boots a complete
local stack â€” Docker Postgres is provisioned automatically, migrations run
under an advisory lock, a default space is created, and a bootstrap key is
printed once. Only the provider API keys (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`,
`OPENAI_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY`) have no default, and only the
one matching `WIKIKIT_LLM_PROVIDER` is read; without it every LLM-free feature
still works. In production the behavior flips: no
auto-provisioning, `DATABASE_URL` and `WIKIKIT_KEY_PEPPER` are mandatory.

`WIKIKIT_OAUTH_PROVIDERS` is a JSON array with unique `id`, `protocol`, and
`label`. API-key records select a product-local key policy; direct OIDC records
add HTTPS `issuer_url`, `client_id`, optional `client_secret`,
`scopes` (including `openid`), `allowed_subjects` and/or `allowed_emails`, and
optional identity-policy overrides. At least one subject or email entry is
required; subject matching is exact and email matching requires
`email_verified=true`. Register
`${WIKIKIT_PUBLIC_URL}/v1/identity/login/callback` with each OIDC provider. Keep
client secrets in the production secret store, never in the repository.

---

## 12. Running & deploying

- **Dev:** `bun run dev` (Bun runs TypeScript natively; no build step).
- **Single binary:** `bun run build:binary` â†’ `dist/wikikit` (a true
  `bun build --compile` executable; no unpack step, no Node on the host).
  Ops flags only â€” this is not a CLI product: no args starts the server,
  `--migrate` migrates and exits, `--version` prints the version.
- **Self-migrating boot:** every start (and `--migrate`) applies the embedded
  SQL migrations under a Postgres advisory lock before HTTP opens; a migration
  failure keeps `/ready` down so the deploy health gate rolls back.

Lifecycle: `/health` (liveness), `/ready` (readiness â€” `{status, version}`;
deploy gates match **both** fields; 503 while draining). On SIGTERM/SIGINT the
service fails `/ready`, stops the workers, drains in-flight requests, then
exits 0. Logs are structured JSON on stdout with request ids; API keys are
never logged.

**Checks:** `bun run gate` runs everything CI runs (~60s, needs Docker) and
installs as a `pre-push` hook via `bun run hooks:install`. Five tiers, each
covering what the one below cannot: `test/unit` (logic + the drift gates that
keep these docs true), `test/contract` (snapshots foreign systems consume),
`test/integration` (real Postgres, FakeProvider), `test/e2e` (the REAL AI SDK
against a stub Anthropic endpoint â€” the only tier that sees the vendor edge:
request shape, `cache_control` placement, usage mapping), and `benchmarks/`
(`bun run bench` â€” reports, never gates; the cost regression that does gate is
`test/unit/prompt-budget.test.ts`).

---

## 13. Integration (standards only)

WikiKit integrates **only over open standards** (OpenAPI/REST, MCP, Standard
Webhooks, OKF, llms.txt) â€” no SDK, no shared code, no direct dependencies. Any
external system consumes it the same way:

- **OpenAPI/REST** â€” `/openapi.json` is a ready-to-import connector for the full API.
- **MCP** â€” federate `/mcp` (Streamable HTTP) to give an agent search/read/propose and permission-gated review tools.
- **Standard Webhooks** â€” subscribe to `wikikit.proposal.created`, `proposal.approved`, `concept.updated`, `ingest.failed`.
- **OKF / Markdown tree** â€” portable knowledge import and export.

Typical seams: a governance layer imports the OpenAPI connector or federates
the MCP server and reacts to `wikikit.proposal.created` with an approval
workflow; a publishing pipeline reads an approved concept and posts a curated
projection out as an article; that published article is re-ingested by URL and
becomes a citable source.

---

## 14. Troubleshooting

- **401 unauthorized** â€” key missing/revoked or the server's
  `WIKIKIT_KEY_PEPPER` changed (hashes no longer match).
- **403 insufficient_scope** â€” key lacks the scope, or a space-scoped key
  touched a foreign space.
- **409 already_ingested** â€” the exact content was ingested before; the
  envelope carries the existing `source_id`. Not an error for retries â€” the
  pipeline is idempotent by design.
- **409 stale_base on approve** â€” the concept changed since the proposal was
  created; re-ingest or re-propose against the current revision.
- **503 llm_not_configured** â€” set the key the error names (the one for the
  configured `WIKIKIT_LLM_PROVIDER`); all LLM-free routes
  keep working meanwhile.
- **Webhook endpoint stopped receiving** â€” check
  `GET .../webhooks/{id}/deliveries`; after repeated failures the circuit
  breaker pauses the endpoint for 15 minutes.
- **MCP client loops on session errors** â€” re-initialize on JSON-RPC `-32001`;
  sessions expire after `WIKIKIT_MCP_SESSION_TTL_MS` idle.
- **MCP review returns `human_review_required`** â€” the client cannot show the
  native review form. The proposal stays pending; the agent gives the user
  the returned `review_url` (the `GET /review/{id}` page) where the human
  decides as themselves; the agent polls `wikikit_proposals`. Never approve
  via chat, tool arguments, or REST calls made on the human's behalf.
- **MCP review returns `approval_requires_human`** â€” the agent passed
  `decision`/`note` as tool input. The tool takes only `proposal_id`; WikiKit
  collects the decision from the human, never from the agent.
